commits.message: fix(CL-w5d): address cold review advisory findings F1 and F2

tests.result: 583 passed, 23 skipped, 2 deselected (after codex adversarial fixes; deselects are pre-existing TestMigrationCommit failures unrelated to dry-run contract)

scope_changes.description: Fixed an existing indentation error in scripts/lib/installers/guardrail_installer.py because guardrail dry-run import failed before AC2 and AC3 behavior could execute.
scope_changes.reason: Required to verify and satisfy guardrail dry-run contract coverage.

scope_changes.description: Cold review advisory F1: threaded conflict_policy parameter into _annotate_existing_targets in scripts/lib/output.py so the dry-run operation suffix renders the actual policy instead of a hardcoded 'overwrite' literal.
scope_changes.reason: Addresses cold review advisory finding F1 — prevents misleading suffix when an installer adopts a non-overwrite policy.

scope_changes.description: Cold review advisory F2: extended the harness_routing JSON Schema enum in docs/schema/dry-run-contract.md to include 'opencode' alongside claude_code, codex, all, and null.
scope_changes.reason: Addresses cold review advisory finding F2 — schema now matches the harness values supported by agent.py and mcp_installer.py.

scope_changes.description: Codex adversarial REGRESSION 1: changed _mcp_config_path in scripts/lib/installers/mcp_installer.py to always return the global harness config path (honoring CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE, CODEX_CONFIG_FILE, OPENCODE_CONFIG_FILE env overrides). The previous project-scope branch produced dry-run target_paths that did not match the real install (install-mcp.py only writes to global config files).
scope_changes.reason: Aligns dry-run target_paths with actual install behavior — satisfies CL-w5d AK 'non-dry-run behavior matches dry-run (no divergence)'.

scope_changes.description: Codex adversarial REGRESSION 2: changed _guardrail_config_path in scripts/lib/installers/guardrail_installer.py to always return the global harness config path (honoring CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE, CODEX_HOOKS_FILE env overrides). The previous project-scope branch projected paths that install-hook.py never writes to.
scope_changes.reason: Aligns dry-run target_paths with actual install behavior — satisfies the same AK as REGRESSION 1.

scope_changes.description: Codex adversarial REGRESSION 3: extended _selected_mcp_harnesses in scripts/lib/installers/mcp_installer.py to enumerate every declared harness in the entry's install.mcp block (including claude_ai and claude_ios), mirroring install-mcp.py main() which iterates over list(mcp_block.keys()) for --harness all. URL-only harnesses surface an install_mcp_server operation with path=null and a 'no file write' detail, and are omitted from target_paths because no file is written.
scope_changes.reason: Restores parity with the real install — previously --harness all dry-run silently misrouted entries that declared only claude_ai/claude_ios as if they targeted claude_code.

scope_changes.description: Codex adversarial REGRESSION 4: changed install_simple_file in scripts/lib/installers/simple_file.py to emit harness_routing=null instead of echoing the --harness argument. resolve_install_paths does not consult harness for simple-file primitives (prompt, script, model-standard, agent-base) — the install target is the same regardless of harness. Aligns with the dry-run contract: 'Installers without harness-specific routing emit null.'
scope_changes.reason: Eliminates misleading harness_routing values for harness-agnostic primitive installers.

scope_changes.description: Updated test_target_project_scope_routes_targets_to_explicit_project in tests/test_library_py_installers.py to use contract-skill instead of contract-guardrail. The original test asserted that --target-project routed guardrail target_paths into the explicit project, but that contradicts the actual install (guardrails always write to global harness config files). The corrected test now exercises a primitive (skill) whose installer genuinely honors --target-project.
scope_changes.reason: Required to keep test coverage of --target-project routing while removing the assertion that encoded REGRESSION 2's broken behavior.

downstream_impacts: All dry-run JSON envelopes now include summary, target_paths, harness_routing, conflict_policy, lockfile_changes, and requires_user_confirmation.
Existing target detection annotates matching dry-run operations with existing_target: true while keeping the current overwrite behavior.

MCP and guardrail dry-runs now report the actual global harness config paths the real install writes to (regardless of --scope project), matching install-mcp.py/install-hook.py behavior. Env-var overrides (CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE, CODEX_CONFIG_FILE, CODEX_HOOKS_FILE, OPENCODE_CONFIG_FILE) are honored in both dry-run and real install.

MCP dry-run with --harness all now enumerates every harness declared in install.mcp (including claude_ai/claude_ios URL-only harnesses), matching install-mcp.py main(). URL-only harnesses appear in the operations list with path=null and are omitted from target_paths since no file is written.
Simple-file installers (prompt, script, model-standard, agent-base) emit harness_routing=null in dry-run JSON because the install target does not vary by harness.
